title: ranunculus lappaceus var. normalis
intro: Ranunculus lappaceus var. normalis, also known as the buttercup, is a perennial plant native to North America and parts of the western United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and compound leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus lappaceus var. normalis include its ability to reduce swelling and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
